title: member_counts_for_user uses correlated subquery; O(N*M) on large group sets
description: relay/src/store.rs member_counts_for_user: SELECT gm.group_id, COUNT(*) FROM group_members gm WHERE gm.group_id IN (SELECT group_id FROM group_members WHERE pub_id = ?1) GROUP BY gm.group_id. The IN subquery runs once per outer row evaluation in SQLite without the query planner rewriting it. For a user in 256 groups each with 100 members, this scans 25,600 rows. SQLite should handle this with an index on group_id, but if the query planner does not use the index for the subquery, it degrades to full scan. Should be rewritten as an explicit JOIN: SELECT gm.group_id, COUNT(*) FROM group_members gm JOIN group_members me ON gm.group_id = me.group_id WHERE me.pub_id = ?1 GROUP BY gm.group_id. This is provably index-friendly on both group_id columns.
